mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the threshold, or signal loses predictive power over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization and operational efficiency, which boosts short-term equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns turn negative in >50% of months following industrial production acceleration, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 3.5% threshold forces central banks to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity multiples, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >65% of months following inflation breaches, or the threshold fails to separate positive and negative return regimes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth in multivariate tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly increases corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters following positive GDP revisions, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling test windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals immediate supply-side contraction and reduced factory output, which triggers short-term risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >55% of instances following the production decline threshold, or the signal fails to maintain statistical significance in high-frequency backtests.
